feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: #CoE#TFAL Treatment of Opening Balances After Historical Balance Migration
entry.content:
(S/4HANA Public Cloud – Fiscal Year April to March)

To align expectations on how opening balances are handled in S/4HANA Public Cloud, please see the explanation below based on our migration and posting approach.

Fiscal Year Definition

  • Fiscal Year runs April to March
    • Example: FY2025 = April 2024 to March 2025

FY2025 – Historical Balance Migration

  • Periods 1 to 12 of FY2025 were migrated using the Historical Balance migration object.
  • The Historical Balance object posts period‑specific balances but automatically reverses the cumulative balance at year‑end.
  • As a result:
    • The cumulative balance at the end of FY2025 is zero
    • No balance is carried forward to the next fiscal year

Impact:

  • The opening balance of FY2026 is zero, which is expected system behavior when Historical Balance is used for a full fiscal year.

FY2026 – Transition Year Approach

  • Periods 1 to 5 (FY2026):
    • Migrated using Historical Balance in the Migration Cockpit
  • Period 6 (FY2026):
    • Migrated using GL Balance and Open Items objects to establish a proper carry‑forward base
  • Periods 7 to 12 (FY2026):
    • Posted through normal business transactions

Impact:

  • From this point onward, balances are system‑driven and continuous.

Year‑End Carryforward (FY2026 → FY2027)

  • At the end of FY2026:
    • Balance Sheet accounts will be carried forward automatically to FY2027 opening balances
    • P&L accounts will be closed and carried forward to Retained Earnings
  • This restores standard SAP year‑end behavior from FY2026 onwards.

Is this a best‑practice approach for S/4HANA Public Cloud implementations?

entry.title: #CoE#TFAL_S/4 HANA public cloud 2602 connection /integration to Sailpoint
entry.content:
Dear All ,

Could you please inform, if any references for S/4 HANA public cloud Projects who has integrated with SailPoint (Either Direct via Sailpoint connectors Identity 8.4/8.5 Connectors Or via other API integration with SAP IAS etc. ).  We are seeing many documents/links on Sailpoint site but no clear information on SAP portals.

Please provide any guide which has steps/activities to follow on S/4 public cloud side to be able to integrate with Sailpoint. 

Thankyou !

entry.title: How to apply filters and select Plan Category for app Cost Center Review Booklet
entry.content:
You are using the older report apps for Cost Center data such as Cost Centre Plan/Actual.

You are now migrating to the new app Cost Center Review Booklet and want to select Plan Category and apply filters to view data in a similar manner to the older app.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: QR IBAN on Billing Document
entry.content:
Hello,

We would like to use the QR code on the invoice in the accounting area of our Swiss subsidiary. 

Here is a screenshot of the settings we have made for this.

We have different IBANs depending on the currency (EUR/CHF).

Unfortunately, it is not working. The IBAN for the CHF currency is always printed on the form, even if the currency in the document is EUR.

Please help.

entry.title: Not able to check "Used for Billing" checkbox while creating Milestone in Enterprise Projects.
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I have created an Enterprise Project with Project Profile as "Project with Revenue". I am now trying to create a Milestone for Billing Plan in the WBS. I understand in the latest S4HANA Public Edition we can now fetch billing plan from WBS into the Sales order. However, the milestone which I am trying to create have a field "Used for Billing" and by default it marked as "No" and its greyed out. I am not able to find out what is the issue. 

 

 

Things I have already checked and performed. 

  1. Verified the project uses a "Project with Revenue" profile
  2. Confirmed billing element indicator is enabled at the appropriate WBS level
  3. Scope ID 4I9(Project Control-Sales) and 1NT(Project Control-Finance) are activated in the system along with other relevant Scope IDs.
  4. Used app "Project Planning" and "project Briefs" to create Milestones. 
  5. In the Process manager for 4I9, the planned costs and revenue were uploaded before the creation of milestones, so I have done that as well. 

I am pretty sure the resolution would be something which i should know but I am not able to figure out 🙂 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud for enterprise portfolio and project management

entry.title: Using local (Belgian) G/L account numbers vs. YCOA: what’s the exact rule across apps?
entry.content:
Hi all,

We run S/4HANA Public Cloud with YCOA as the operational CoA. We want to work with local (Belgian) G/L numbers for day‑to‑day use (at least for entry/display), while accepting YCOA as the technical posting account.

My understanding:

  • In FI apps where users manually select a G/L, local/alternative accounts can be used for selection/display (e.g., Post General Journal Entries via Edit Options for Journal Entries → Alternative Account).
  • In system-determined scenarios (MM-driven postings, AP/AR operational apps, uploads/APIs), postings run on YCOA; local numbers may appear in value help but are not the operational posting account.
  • Example: In Create Supplier Invoice, Offsetting Account value help shows both local and YCOA numbers, but after selection the app posts/displays YCOA.

Questions:
Could you please provide a concise list or rule-of-thumb matrix of where local/alternative accounts are usable (entry/display) and where they are not in Public Cloud—beyond Post General Journal Entries (e.g., accruals, reclassification, asset postings without PO, etc.)? Representative examples are sufficient; no need for an exhaustive tile-by-tile list.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Tax rate not calculating for intergration with vertex tax engine
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am testing Vertex tax integration for Sales Scheduling Agreements in our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

We have two finished product (FERT) materials with similar attributes:

Material A – created manually by the procurement/MM team

Material B – created via data migration templates by the Data Migration team

When I create a Scheduling Agreement with Tax Category O1:

For Material A, the system determines tax correctly and returns 6% in condition type XR1 in the Conditions tab.

For Material B, the XR1 condition is empty, and no tax percentage is determined.

To further verify, I duplicated the manually created material (for example, Material 69) and created a new material (699) with the same attributes. When I used this duplicated material in the Scheduling Agreement, the system again returned 6% in XR1.

However, when using the data-migrated material, the XR1 condition remains empty.

I also checked the Vertex payload, where the material is marked as non-taxable, and therefore no tax percentage is returned.

I suspect that there might be some field differences between the manually created material and the migrated material, as the migrated material was created using data templates and some fields related to tax determination might have been missed.

Could you please advise:

What could cause a migrated material to be treated as non-taxable by Vertex even though it appears to have similar attributes?

Which material fields are required for tax determination when using the Vertex tax engine?

Any suggestions or guidance would be greatly appreciated.


Left one is payload for manaually created material.
Right one is payload for material created using data migration.

Thanks.

entry.title: SO is not created in Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer (5HP)
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

We are using Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer process. In material master availability check "SP" is maintained in receiving plant. When PO is created no SO is created in issuing plant due to no stock. 

The process is issuing plant will start the production most of the times, after PO is created.

Can you suggest possible solution so that SO can be created in issuing plant with Stock of material.

Thanks

Pavan

entry.title: Can all approvers be displayed correctly from the beginning when using BAdI MMPUR_WORKFLOW_AGENTS_V2
entry.content:
I am using Workflow for Purchase Requisition with BAdI MMPUR_WORKFLOW_AGENTS_V2 to determine approvers dynamically.

The workflow has 4 approval levels.

Example approvers:

  • Level 1 → User A

  • Level 2 → User B

  • Level 3 → User C

  • Level 4 → User D

The issue I see is that when the workflow starts (before anyone approves), the approvers are not displayed correctly for all levels.

For example, at the beginning:

  • Level 1 → correct (User A)

  • Level 2 → correct (User B)

  • Level 3 → shows an incorrect approver (sometimes same as level 1)

  • Level 4 → also incorrect

Then once the workflow progresses:

  • after level 2 is approved → level 3 becomes correct

  • after level 3 is approved → level 4 becomes correct

From tracing the BAdI, I can see that initially it is only called for:

 

 
STEPINFO-currentstep = 001
STEPINFO-currentstep = 002
and it is not called for steps 003 or 004 until the workflow actually reaches those steps.

So my question is:

Is it possible to display the correct approvers for all approval levels from the beginning, before any approval step is completed, when using BAdI MMPUR_WORKFLOW_AGENTS_V2?

entry.title: Company Code–Wise Number Range Configuration for PO
entry.content:
Subject: Feasibility Request – Company Code–Wise Number Range Configuration for PO (Public Cloud)

Dear Team,

In our SAP Public Cloud client, we are looking for a company code–wise number range configuration. Kindly confirm whether this requirement is feasible.

Requirement Details:

  • PO number should be generated based on company code–specific number range.
  • Example:
    • Company Code: IN08
    • Required PO Number Format: IN08000000000000

Request you to please evaluate the feasibility and update us at the earliest.

Warm Regards,
P. Ravi Kiran

Attached the screenshot for your reference

entry.title: How to Add "Document Header Text" in Free Selection of Manage Automatic Payments (F110)
entry.content:
Dear SAP，

We'd like to use “Document Header Text” in free selection1 field when we input parameter in “Manage automatic payments” APP to select the AP documents for payment. How can we add “Document Header Text” (BKPF-BKTXT) in free selection1 field?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: I have 6000 supplier records to which I want to add tax numbers - how can I do it?
entry.content:
I'm using Public Cloud. I tried Mass Maintenance, but the import option can only create new records and scope doesn't pick up supplier records with blank tax number. 
In Migration Cockpit there isn't an object to extend suppliers/ business partners for tax number. The only ones I have available are: 
Supplier - extend existing record by new org levels
Supplier - extend existing record by Thailand branch code
Supplier- extend existing record by Ukrainian tax info

How can I import tax number data into 6000 suppliers?

entry.title: Quotation Number Not Consistently Reflected in Purchase Order CDS Views
entry.content:
For a custom development requirement, we need to derive the Supplier Quotation number linked to a Purchase Order.

Currently, we are referring to the following CDS views:

  • I_PURCHASEORDERAPI01 → Field: SUPPLIERQUOTATIONEXTERNALID
  • I_PURCHASEORDERITEMTP_2 → Field: SUPPLIERQUOTATION

However, we are facing an issue where quotation numbers are not consistently populated in these CDS views.
For some Purchase Orders, the quotation number is displayed correctly, but for other POs that do have an associated quotation, the quotation number is not reflected in either of the above CDS views.

Request:

Please help us understand:

  • Why the quotation number is not available for all relevant Purchase Orders in these CDS views.
  • If there is any alternative standard CDS view or field recommended to reliably retrieve the quotation number linked to a Purchase Order.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Automatic Clearing of the pre-paid Withholding tax GL Account for Mexico using F.13 in Public Cloud
entry.content:
For Mexico, withholding tax is charged at the time of payment. Therefore, when the invoice document is posted, the withholding tax amount is initially posted to the Prepaid Withholding Tax GL Account in the journal entry. Subsequently, when the payment is executed, the amount is transferred from the Prepaid Withholding Tax GL Account to the Withholding Tax GL Account.

Please find the relevant accounting entries below for reference.

Invoice document:

Payment Document:

 

We are currently exploring the possibility of automatically clearing the Prepaid Withholding Tax GL Account using F.13. However, we have observed that there is no common clearing criteria between the invoice and the payment document that can be utilized by F.13.

As a potential solution, we considered using the document reference of the invoice document by copying its value into the Assignment field of the Prepaid WHT GL line item. However, we noticed that the same document reference value is not populated in the payment document, even when the payment is executed for a single invoice. In the payment document, the Document Reference field remains blank.

Could you please confirm if you have encountered a similar scenario before, and if there is any recommended approach to enable automatic clearing in this case by copying the same document reference in the payment document.

entry.title: Is there a Migration Object related to Project Hours (Timesheet Entries Posted to Projects / WBS) ?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

 

I’m currently working on a project in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition  and I need clarification regarding the migration of project‑related timesheet hours, specifically hours recorded per person per day and posted to Projects / WBS elements.
 
Based on my research, here is what I have found there is no migration object for project hours (timesheet entries linked to Projects / WBS) in the Migration Cockpit

Could you please confirm whether:

  • There is no migration object in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for importing project/WBS timesheet hours And is there any possibility to upload using a batch or mass upload of timesheet hours per person/ per day?
Thank you in advance

entry.title: Is there a Migration Object related to Project Hours (Timesheet Entries Posted to Projects / WBS) ?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

 

I’m currently working on a project in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition  and I need clarification regarding the migration of project‑related timesheet hours, specifically hours recorded per person per day and posted to Projects / WBS elements.
 
Based on my research, here is what I have found there is no migration object for project hours (timesheet entries linked to Projects / WBS) in the Migration Cockpit

Could you please confirm whether:

  • There is no migration object in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for importing project/WBS timesheet hours, and is there any possibility to upload using a batch or mass upload of timesheet hours per person/ per day?
Thank you in advance

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud SO Item Ship to party Address Change
entry.content:
We would like to have a different delivery address for the ship-to party at sales order item level.

It seems not possible to edit the ship-to party delivery address in sales order item partner function - I find it greyed out. Please help to advise how can I set the sales order item ship-to party address editable.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: ACTIVATION OF COMMINUCATION SETTING API- SAP_COM_0564
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are currently fetching billing details from SAP to a non-SAP system using the API SAP Self-Billing Cockpit Integration (SAP_COM_0564).

To activate this API, we need to complete the communication setup. However, we are currently unable to perform the communication settings.

Could you please assist us in resolving this issue or guide us on the required steps?

Thank you




------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.
